title: utilities.js — getProgressWidth clamps percentage to 100 when value exceeds max
commit_message: fix: cap progress bar width at 100% for over-range values

why_micro_hotfix.explanation: Only getProgressWidth changes. CRITICAL = describe('getProgressWidth') (2 tests). HIGH = every other describe in standalone/utilities.pure.test.js (checkNull, checkArray, checkWhiteSpace, getTimes) — sibling symbols in the same file, mirroring scenario 9 (checkWhiteSpace) and scenario 11 (getTimes).
why_micro_hotfix.key_point: 2 CRITICAL + 8 HIGH = 10 selected; 67 SKIP — no cross-dependent or feature test lists getProgressWidth in symbols[].

tests.test: getProgressWidth > calculates 50% when value is half of max
tests.result: ⚠️  MAY FAIL — if test passes value > max expecting unclamped width

tests.test: getProgressWidth > returns 0 when the value argument is missing or falsy

HIGH.reason: Same source file utilities.js — sibling symbols

tests.test: checkNull > returns an empty string when the value is null
checkNull > returns the original value when it is defined and non-null
checkArray > returns an empty array when the input is null
checkArray > returns the original array when it contains items
checkWhiteSpace > returns true for a string that contains a space
checkWhiteSpace > returns false for a string with no whitespace
getTimes > returns 0 hours when the duration is under 3600 seconds

SKIP.reason: utilities.js not paired with getProgressWidth in cross-dependent or feature manifests for this selection policy

coverage_gaps.detail: Add a test that value=200, max=100 yields width 100 (clamped) if that is the new contract.
